issues.title: Add text in 2.2 that explains we don't need to worry about SID 0
issues.author: cabo
issues.body: (Should that be "A" or ""? We don't need to say, as SID 0 is not for interchange.)

comments.author: abierman
comments.body:
SID 0 cannot show up as a CBOR key.
Not as an absolute or a delta. (Impossible as a delta).

SID as a value can be 0.  The sid typedef starts at 0.

issues.title: Response code for RPC/Action needs to be 2.04 (Changed)
issues.author: cabo
issues.body:
Section 3.5 says that the response code for RPC/Action is 2.05.
That doesn't mesh with RFC 7252, which has 2.04 for this specific case.
The examples in 3.5, 3.5.1, and 3.5.2 are correct (say 2.04), only the text says 2.05.

(reported by Michel Veillette)

(The title of 3.5 could also be updated to "RPC and Action Statements".)

issues.title: Section 2.3: Examples of each media type would be helpful here
issues.author: cabo

issues.title: Do we need RESTCONF depth/fields filters?
issues.author: cabo
issues.body:
(Andy Bierman, <https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/core/XLv4ZusZubBrJPC5aW7UqqeHrxw>)

The client must know all of the key values in advance
before being able to use the SID of a data node
in a FETCH request.  It is difficult and expensive
to retrieve the entire datastore contents at once,
just to discover the key values in use for the YANG list
entries within the datastore.

RESTCONF has the 'depth' and 'fields' filter parameters
to address this issue. NETCONF has subtree and XPath filtering.
NETCONF servers can generally support selection of 1 value or all
values, for each key.

Perhaps CORECONF will need the 'depth' filter parameter.

comments.author: abierman
comments.body:
Follow up: OK to defer to future

- The client does not need to know all the keys in advance to get all the list entries, by getting an ancestor of the list
-  The amount of data retrieved depends on the YANG modules used. It is possible to design containers with limited
   child nodes and depth. It is possible to limit the maximum number of list instances, or expect a small number of entries by design,
-  The 'depth' parameter is not likely to help in CoMI because the data trees will not be as complex or deep as NETCONF
-  These filtering query parameters are optional in RESTCONF

The likely problem is that the list is too wide, not too deep.
Even a simple list with 4 leafs in it is a lot of data if there are 10000 entries in the list.
If needed, a list pagination mechanism could be added in the future.
NETCONF WG has work-in-progress: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-netconf-list-pagination/

comments.author: lemikev
comments.body:
For now, getting indexes, pagination, and other approaches can be implemented using RPCs.
This can be sufficient even for the long term.

Michel

issues.title: Add examples for RPC/action without input parameters
issues.author: cabo

issues.title: Consistent Usage of SID number in Examples
issues.author: vvilimek
issues.body:
From [ML email sent by PABLO HERNANDEZ GALINDO](https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/core/l7JezHjsKZaEsT0qt2LZIldBw-E/)

> Consistency in SID Examples While studying the draft, I found it somewhat difficult to follow the examples because the SIDs and data models change between different sections without a clear continuity. As an implementer, it would greatly improve readability if the document followed a cohesive sequence, using the same SIDs (e.g., from a single, consistent YANG module) to demonstrate GET, FETCH, iPATCH, etc., throughout the text.

issues.title: »null« is a valid data item in YANG/CBOR; need to do indicate absent/removed nodes differently
issues.author: cabo
issues.body:
null is used in an iPATCH to remove a data node or in a FETCH to indicate the absence of a data node.

However, in RFC 9254 (Encoding of Data Modeled with YANG in CBOR), null is a valid data node value.
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9254#section-6.11

This issue has been raised last year and not yet resolved.
The fix consists of replacing all instances of null in draft-ietf-core-comi by undefined.

undefined is CBOR value F7, null is CBOR value F6
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8949#section-5.7

(Via Michel Veillette)

pulls.title: Changes from IETF 115 Hackathon
pulls.author: cabo
pulls.body:
This PR reflects the discussions at the IETF 115 Hackathon and at the [2023-01-18 CoRE interim][1].

- Simplifies the outer arrays to CBOR sequences (RFC 8742).
- Fixes the comma-separation ambiguity in the URI Query parameter.
- Gets rid of the complexity of (old) Table 6.

[1]: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/minutes-interim-2023-core-01-202301181500/#coap-management-interface-comi

This PR is planned to be submitted as -12, for further discussion in the WG.

pulls.title: YANG Data Structure Extension
pulls.author: vvilimek
pulls.body:
Is there a reason for using the `rc:yang-data` of RESTCONF [[RFC 8040]](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8040.html) YANG `ietf-restconf` module? I think usage of `sx:structure` statement of YANG Data Structure Extension [[RFC 8791]](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8791.html) (from YANG `ietf-yang-structure-ext` module) would be beneficial for future extensibility of the draft.

On the other hand the legacy `rc:yang-data` is the same construct used by RESTCONF.

comments.author: abierman
comments.body:
The CoMI draft was first published in 2017, 3 years before RFC 8791.
Usage of rc:yang-data is now considered deprecated, and sx:structure is used instead.
No objections to this change since YANG doctors will flag this issue anyway.

comments.author: cabo
comments.body:
Thank you.
Of course, by now we also have RFC 9290, so it is not quite clear to me which of these we should actually use.
I merged this base on Andy's comment on YANG doctors, but I'm not sure what the level of disturbance is that this creates.

comments.author: cabo
comments.body: I'll need to fix the CI because there have been some changes in Martin Thomson's template.
